headline: Exclusive-Chevron, Shell closing in on first big oil production deals in Venezuela since US captured Maduro, sources say
summary: HOUSTON/CARACAS, March 10 (Reuters) - International oil majors Chevron and Shell are closing in on the first big oil production deals with Venezuela since the U. capture of President Nicolas Maduro in January, five sources close to the negotiations told Reuters.

headline: Oil Prices Bounce As Iran Blockade Holds; S&P 500 Slips
summary: Oil prices dived below $80 a barrel Tuesday afternoon, then rebounded after Energy Secretary Chris Wright deleted his post claiming that the U.S. Navy "successfully escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz."  The S&P 500 relinquished gains on the false claim and turned slightly lower.  The White House later confirmed that Wright's post was false.

The S&P 500 relinquished gains on the false claim and turned slightly lower.  The White House later confirmed that Wright's post was false.

headline: Oil Prices Fall On Reserve News, Despite Latest Iran Threat; S&P 500 Rallies
summary: Oil prices rose back above $90 a barrel but reversed lower later on Tuesday morning, just a hint of Monday's volatility that saw oil spike to $119 then plunge as President Donald Trump provided assurance that the war would end very soon.  Iran pushed back on Tuesday morning, vowing to prevent "the export of even a single liter of oil from the region to the hostile side and its partners until further notice," the Associated Press reported.

headline: Oil Prices Slosh Higher On Iran Threat; S&P 500 Slips
summary: Oil prices rose back to around $90 a barrel after retreating to $85 yesterday from an intraday peak near $120 as President Donald Trump provided assurance that the war would end very soon.  Iran pushed back on Tuesday morning, vowing to prevent "the export of even a single liter of oil from the region to the hostile side and its partners until further notice," the Associated Press reported.  Since then, Iran has launched new missile and drone attacks on Israel, the United Arab Emirates and Qatar.

Iran pushed back on Tuesday morning, vowing to prevent "the export of even a single liter of oil from the region to the hostile side and its partners until further notice," the Associated Press reported.  Since then, Iran has launched new missile and drone attacks on Israel, the United Arab Emirates and Qatar.
